task_completion.description: Verify that the 'SYNTHETIC (Live)' data source is selected in the OpenBCI GUI.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenBCI GUI System Control panel shows 'SYNTHETIC' or 'Synthetic (Live)' selected as the active data source.

task_completion.description: Verify that the recording format is set to BDF+.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dropdown, toggle, or setting in the OpenBCI GUI (typically in the System Control panel or session settings) shows 'BDF+' or 'BDF' selected as the recording format.

task_completion.description: Verify that the data stream and recording have been started.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GUI shows active data streaming (e.g., scrolling EEG waveforms) and a 'Stop Data Stream' or 'Stop System' button is visible, indicating the session is active.

task_completion.description: Verify that the recording lasted for at least 5 seconds.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GUI timer, time series x-axis, or recording duration indicator shows a time of 00:00:05 or greater while recording.

task_completion.description: Verify that a .bdf file was saved to the default recordings directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager or terminal window shows a file with a '.bdf' extension located in the directory '/home/ga/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Recordings'.

integrity.description: The agent used the actual OpenBCI GUI application to configure and record the data.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the OpenBCI GUI interface being interacted with, rather than just terminal commands or scripts mocking the file creation.

integrity.description: The .bdf file was generated by the GUI, not manually created via terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal history (if visible) does not show commands like 'touch file.bdf' or 'echo > file.bdf'. The file size should be non-zero (typically ~36KB for 5 seconds of data).

integrity.description: The agent did not use a text editor or hex editor to manually construct a fake BDF file.
integrity.visual_evidence: No text editors (like nano, vim, gedit) or hex editors are seen open with BDF header signatures (e.g., manually typing 'BIOSEMI').

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected file extension is '.bdf'. The default recordings directory is '/home/ga/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Recordings'. A valid BDF file header starts with byte 0xFF followed by 'BIOSEMI' in ASCII. The recording must last at least 5 seconds. A genuine 5-second BDF recording from OpenBCI is typically around 36KB in size.
